alcohol|metformin.mechanism: additive effect on lactate metabolism and inhibition of gluconeogenesis by alcohol
alcohol|metformin.effect: increased risk of lactic acidosis and hypoglycemia
alcohol|metformin.recommendation: avoid excessive alcohol consumption while taking metformin

apixaban|aspirin.effect: significantly increased risk of major gastrointestinal and systemic bleeding
apixaban|aspirin.recommendation: avoid combination unless specifically indicated; monitor for signs of bleeding

apixaban|ibuprofen.mechanism: additive anticoagulant effect and NSAID-induced platelet dysfunction with gastric mucosal injury
apixaban|ibuprofen.effect: significantly increased risk of gastrointestinal bleeding
apixaban|ibuprofen.recommendation: avoid concurrent use of NSAIDs; use paracetamol for pain relief if appropriate

aspirin|ibuprofen.mechanism: competitive COX-1 inhibition by ibuprofen blocks aspirin binding
aspirin|ibuprofen.effect: reduced cardioprotective effect of low-dose aspirin and increased GI side effects
aspirin|ibuprofen.recommendation: take aspirin at least 30 minutes before or 8 hours after ibuprofen

aspirin|rivaroxaban.effect: significantly increased risk of major gastrointestinal and systemic bleeding
aspirin|rivaroxaban.recommendation: avoid combination unless specifically indicated; monitor for bleeding

aspirin|warfarin.mechanism: additive antiplatelet effect, anticoagulant effect, and direct gastric mucosal injury
aspirin|warfarin.effect: major increase in gastrointestinal and systemic bleeding risk
aspirin|warfarin.recommendation: avoid concurrent use unless clinically indicated (e.g. mechanical valve); monitor INR closely

atorvastatin|clarithromycin.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition by clarithromycin leading to increased atorvastatin concentration
atorvastatin|clarithromycin.effect: increased statin levels leading to muscle toxicity and potential rhabdomyolysis
atorvastatin|clarithromycin.recommendation: avoid combination; temporarily hold atorvastatin or use alternative antibiotic

calcium|levothyroxine.mechanism: calcium binds levothyroxine in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing its absorption
calcium|levothyroxine.effect: reduced efficacy of levothyroxine, leading to subtherapeutic thyroid hormone levels
calcium|levothyroxine.recommendation: separate administration times of calcium and levothyroxine by at least 4 hours

clarithromycin|warfarin.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition and disruption of gut flora by clarithromycin
clarithromycin|warfarin.effect: significantly increased warfarin levels, elevated INR, and high bleeding risk
clarithromycin|warfarin.recommendation: avoid concurrent use; use alternative antibiotic or monitor INR daily

ciprofloxacin|warfarin.mechanism: alteration of gut flora and potential inhibition of CYP1A2/CYP3A4 by ciprofloxacin
ciprofloxacin|warfarin.effect: increased anticoagulant effect of warfarin and elevated bleeding risk
ciprofloxacin|warfarin.recommendation: monitor INR closely and adjust warfarin dose if necessary

clopidogrel|omeprazole.mechanism: CYP2C19 inhibition by omeprazole reduces the activation of clopidogrel
clopidogrel|omeprazole.effect: reduced antiplatelet efficacy of clopidogrel and increased risk of cardiovascular events
clopidogrel|omeprazole.recommendation: avoid concurrent use; consider using pantoprazole or another PPI that does not inhibit CYP2C19

diclofenac|ibuprofen.effect: increased risk of gastrointestinal ulceration, bleeding, and renal impairment
diclofenac|ibuprofen.recommendation: avoid dual NSAID therapy; use alternative non-NSAID analgesics if possible

diclofenac|losartan.mechanism: NSAID-induced prostaglandin inhibition decreases renal perfusion and antagonizes vasodilatory effects
diclofenac|losartan.effect: reduced antihypertensive efficacy and increased risk of acute kidney injury
diclofenac|losartan.recommendation: monitor blood pressure and renal function; ensure patient is hydrated

diclofenac|telmisartan.mechanism: NSAID-induced prostaglandin inhibition decreases renal perfusion and antagonizes vasodilatory effects
diclofenac|telmisartan.effect: reduced antihypertensive efficacy and increased risk of acute kidney injury
diclofenac|telmisartan.recommendation: monitor blood pressure and renal function; ensure patient is hydrated

doxycycline|warfarin.mechanism: doxycycline decreases vitamin K-producing gut flora and may inhibit warfarin metabolism
doxycycline|warfarin.recommendation: monitor INR during co-administration and adjust warfarin dose as needed

ibuprofen|losartan.mechanism: NSAID-induced prostaglandin inhibition decreases renal perfusion and antagonizes vasodilatory effects
ibuprofen|losartan.effect: reduced antihypertensive efficacy and increased risk of acute kidney injury
ibuprofen|losartan.recommendation: monitor blood pressure and renal function; ensure patient is hydrated

ibuprofen|rivaroxaban.mechanism: NSAID-induced platelet dysfunction and gastric mucosal damage combined with anticoagulation
ibuprofen|rivaroxaban.effect: significantly increased risk of gastrointestinal bleeding
ibuprofen|rivaroxaban.recommendation: avoid concurrent use of NSAIDs; use paracetamol for pain relief if appropriate

ibuprofen|telmisartan.mechanism: NSAID-induced prostaglandin inhibition decreases renal perfusion and antagonizes vasodilatory effects
ibuprofen|telmisartan.effect: reduced antihypertensive efficacy and increased risk of acute kidney injury
ibuprofen|telmisartan.recommendation: monitor blood pressure and renal function; ensure patient is hydrated

iron|levothyroxine.mechanism: iron supplements form insoluble complexes with levothyroxine in the gut, reducing absorption
iron|levothyroxine.effect: reduced absorption of levothyroxine, leading to hypothyroidism symptoms
iron|levothyroxine.recommendation: separate administration times of iron and levothyroxine by at least 4 hours

metformin|contrast dye.mechanism: contrast dye induced renal impairment increases metformin levels
metformin|contrast dye.effect: increased risk of metformin-induced lactic acidosis
metformin|contrast dye.recommendation: temporarily discontinue metformin prior to or at the time of the procedure and withhold for 48 hours after

metronidazole|warfarin.mechanism: CYP2C9 inhibition by metronidazole decreases S-warfarin metabolism
metronidazole|warfarin.effect: significantly increased warfarin levels, elevated INR, and severe bleeding risk
metronidazole|warfarin.recommendation: avoid concurrent use if possible; if used together, reduce warfarin dose and monitor INR closely

paracetamol|warfarin.mechanism: prolonged high-dose paracetamol may inhibit warfarin metabolism or affect clotting factor synthesis
paracetamol|warfarin.effect: gradual increase in INR and bleeding risk with prolonged use of high-dose paracetamol
paracetamol|warfarin.recommendation: limit paracetamol dose and monitor INR closely for patients on chronic therapy

simvastatin|clarithromycin.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition by clarithromycin leading to increased simvastatin concentration
simvastatin|clarithromycin.effect: high risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis
simvastatin|clarithromycin.recommendation: contraindicated; temporarily hold simvastatin or use alternative antibiotic

warfarin|ibuprofen.mechanism: platelet inhibition by ibuprofen and anticoagulation by warfarin
warfarin|ibuprofen.effect: increased gastrointestinal and systemic bleeding risk
warfarin|ibuprofen.recommendation: avoid combination; use alternative analgesics like paracetamol
